sections.paragraph:
ἀδιαλείπτως adv. (since Metrodorus Philos. [IV/III b.c.]: PHercul 831, 8; Polyb. 9, 3, 8; SIG 1104, 15; 1171, 4; pap fr. II b.c. on [Mayser 458]; 1, 2, 3 Macc; EpArist 92; 294; Aristobulus in Eus., PE 13, 12, 4; TestLevi 13:2; TestSol 3:7; Jos., Bell. 3, 164; 241; Just., D. 133, 6) constantly, unceasingly μνείαν ποιεῖσθαι Ro 1:9; 1 Th 1:2. εὐχαριστεῖν 2:13. προσεύχεσθαι 5:17; IEph 10:1; Hs 9, 11, 7; cp. Pol 4:3. προσκαρτερεῖν τῇ ἐλπίδι Pol 8:1. διδόναι (of God) Hs 5, 4, 4 v.l. (for ἀδιστάκτως). σκεπάζειν Hs 9, 27, 2.—DELG s.v. λείπω.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Metrodorus Philos Metrodorus Philos , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

  1. fr. = from

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 20
